questions.question: Which standard of the Care Certificate asks you to put equality, diversity and inclusion at the heart of your practice?
In the FREDA principles, what does the letter 'D' stand for?
Which Act gives everyone in the UK a set of basic rights simply because they are human, and requires public bodies to respect them?
Which body enforces the Equality Act 2010?
options: The Equality and Human Rights Commission (EHRC)

questions.question: What does the idea of 'intersectionality' recognise?
options: That a person's overlapping identities combine to shape their experience
That people can only be defined by one protected characteristic
That everyone should be treated in exactly the same way

questions.question: What is the first of the five steps in the Accessible Information Standard?
Which statement best describes the protected characteristic of gender reassignment under the Equality Act 2010?
options: Being married or in a civil partnership
Being attracted to the same or opposite sex
Being pregnant or having recently given birth
Proposing to undergo, undergoing or having undergone a process to reassign one's sex

questions.question: Under the Equality Act 2010, the protected characteristic of race includes which of the following?
options: Colour, nationality and ethnic or national origin

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a reasonable adjustment in everyday care?
options: Providing information in easy-read, large print or braille
Expecting a person to manage without any communication aids
Requiring everyone to follow an identical daily routine
Refusing to change mealtimes for any reason

questions.question: What is the role of an independent advocate?
options: To make all decisions on the person's behalf
To help a person understand their rights and speak up for what they want
To provide the person's medical treatment

questions.question: Under the Equality Act 2010, the protected characteristic 'religion or belief':
options: only covers the major world religions

questions.question: In the FREDA principles, what does the letter 'A' stand for?
Which regulator expects providers to deliver care that is fair, person-centred and free from discrimination?
options: The Equality and Human Rights Commission (EHRC)

questions.question: How does the Equality Act 2010 define 'disability'?
options: Being a particular age or age group
A short-term illness that soon passes
A physical or mental impairment with a long-term effect on everyday activities
Any difference in a person's culture or background

questions.question: The Human Rights Act 1998 means a person's basic human rights can be taken away because of their age or illness.
The Equality Act 2010 brought together many older laws into a single Act.
Making reasonable adjustments for a disabled person is an optional favour rather than a legal duty.
Under the Accessible Information Standard, a person's communication needs should be shared with others involved in their care with the person's consent.
Which of the following are principles in the FREDA framework? (Select all that apply)
Which of the following are steps in the Accessible Information Standard? (Select all that apply)
Which of these are sources of help within your own organisation when you have an EDI question? (Select all that apply)
options: The Equality and Human Rights Commission
Your workplace equality, diversity and inclusion policy

questions.question: If you witness discrimination, which of the following should you do? (Select all that apply)
options: Support the person affected and protect their dignity
Record your personal opinions about who was to blame
Report it to your manager or supervisor
Keep it to yourself to avoid conflict
Challenge it appropriately if it is safe to do so

questions.question: Since 2016, all organisations providing NHS or adult social care in England must follow the Accessible ___ Standard.
___ is the idea that a person's overlapping identities combine to shape their experience in ways unique to them.
The Human Rights Act ___ protects rights such as dignity and freedom from degrading treatment.
